content.problemStatement:
Bob and Alice are playing a game with the following rules:


Initially, they have an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​; in addition, Bob has a lucky number aaa and Alice has a lucky number bbb.
The players alternate turns. In each turn, the current player must remove a non-zero number of elements from the sequence; each removed element should be a multiple of the lucky number of this player.
If it is impossible to remove any elements, the current player loses the game.
It is clear that one player wins the game after a finite number of turns. Find the winner of the game if Bob plays first and both Bob and Alice play optimally.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, a, and b. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string "ALICE" if the winner is Alice or "BOB" if the winner is Bob.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: Bob removes 3 and the sequence becomes [1,2,4,5]. Then, Alice removes 2 and the sequence becomes [1,4,5]. Now, Bob is left with no moves, so Alice is the winner.
Explanation: Example case 2: Bob removes all the numbers and Alice is left with no moves, so Bob is the winner.

Explanation: Example case 3: Bob and Alice have the same lucky number, so they will remove the same numbers. Bob will remove all the numbers and Alice will be left with no moves, but since Bob and Alice have the same lucky number, Alice will win.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a simple game theory approach. We will simulate the game and check who wins. We will use a while loop to simulate the game until one player has no moves left. In each iteration, we will check if the current player can make a move. If they can, we will remove the elements that are multiples of their lucky number and update the sequence. If they cannot, we will end the game and declare the other player the winner.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — explain: per query is O(N), total is O(T * N)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain: we need to store the sequence of numbers
